key_insights.insight: Every moment, whether dark or bright, is temporary, and understanding this can bring happiness and help navigate challenges.
key_insights.quote: It's empowering, it's peace-bringing to understand that every moment we experience is temporary. Every moment. Our darkest moments, our most stressful moments, they are temporary.

key_insights.insight: Trusting your gut instinct, despite compelling evidence to the contrary, is crucial, as it's a survival mechanism honed over millennia.
key_insights.quote: Trust your gut, ladies and gentlemen. I promise you,trust your gut. We don't trust our instincts enough.

key_insights.insight: Our actions define who we are, especially when faced with difficult choices; those decisions reveal our true selves.
key_insights.quote: We are all defined by how we react to hard. When that thought crossesyour mind, this is hard, right? Your decision in that moment is a defining one. That's who you are.

key_insights.insight: True success and happiness come from authenticity and embracing your unique self, rather than trying to emulate others.
key_insights.quote: I genuinely believe that if you want to become great happy successful all you need to do is stop being everyone else ortrying to be someone else

key_insights.insight: Saying 'sorry' and 'thank you' more often, even when not strictly necessary, can significantly improve relationships and foster gratitude.
key_insights.quote: I've come to learn especially over the last 12 months the liberating important value of saying sorry more even when you're not wrong you're just saying sorry because of the way you've made someone feel.

key_insights.insight: Social comparison, especially driven by social media, can lead to feelings of inadequacy and hinder personal growth.
key_insights.quote: The only worthwhile comparison, the only comparison that stands to make your future bright, is the comparison of you today today versus you yesterday.

key_insights.insight: True happiness stems from intrinsic motivations and helping others, rather than chasing external validation or material wealth.
key_insights.quote: People that do things for internal reasons, they are nice because they want to be nice...science shows those people are the most happiest the less depressed the less anxious andthey experience more joy

actionable_advice.advice: Ask yourself 'Who do I want to be?' regularly and align your actions with that ideal.
actionable_advice.how_to: Throughout the day, consciously evaluate whether your current actions (e.g., food choices, work habits) are in line with the person you aspire to be.

actionable_advice.advice: Make a conscious effort to spend time with loved ones, engage in daily exercise, and immerse yourself in nature.
actionable_advice.how_to: Schedule dedicated time for social activities, physical activity, and outdoor experiences, treating them as non-negotiable appointments.

actionable_advice.advice: Prioritize saying 'sorry' and 'thank you' to improve relationships.
actionable_advice.how_to: Make a habit of expressing apologies for any hurt caused, even unintentionally, and actively seek opportunities to express gratitude, even for small gestures.

memorable_stories: Story of a young man contemplating suicide after comparing his life to others on social media.
Anecdote about the video 'Smiling Through Depression' and its unexpected connection to a suicide.
The author's personal experience with being overweight in 2018 and the realization that fitness is a key to happiness.
contrarian_claims: Human beings weren't necessarily built to have mental health issues. Societal and cultural factors likely play a major role.
The author contends Social Chain is the #1 social marketing agency despite admitting he hasn't fact checked it
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E21: Mental Health, Mistakes and My Apologies
